critic.verdict: A well-structured Easy chart with comfortable flow and good density tracking, though it suffers from a highly repetitive chorus and minor off-beat syncopations.

issues.problem: The hold starts on an off-beat (8th note) at 27.712, which breaks the established quarter-note rhythm and may confuse beginner players.
An isolated 8th-note step occurs here, transitioning immediately into a hold at 38.968, which is slightly too complex for an Easy difficulty.
The 4-beat pattern (Jump LR -> D -> U -> L) is repeated almost 7 times consecutively without any variation, making the main chorus feel overly repetitive and mechanical.

critic.verdict: The chart is highly repetitive, copy-pasting a single asymmetric pattern for almost the entire main section, leading to a tedious and unbalanced gameplay experience.

issues.problem: Extreme repetitiveness. The 'U -> D -> L -> JUMP LR' loop is repeated continuously for over 35 seconds with almost no variation, making the main chorus incredibly boring and lazy.

Asymmetric and awkward foot flow. The repeated pattern forces either an awkward double-step into the jump, or an unbalanced stance where the left foot does most of the lateral movement while the right foot remains static.
Isolated 16th notes. Tapping D at 63.354 right before a JUMP LR at 63.479 creates an abrupt, uncomfy one-footed jump transition that is not introduced anywhere else in the chart.
The density collapses completely in the outro, leaving very sparse, disconnected notes that drag on without satisfyingly matching the fading energy of the track.

critic.verdict: A highly repetitive chart with severe flow issues, dominated by a looping pattern that forces awkward double-steps and an uncreative copy-pasted jump section.

issues.problem: The stream is heavily dominated by a looping 'D -> U -> R -> L' pattern (and its rotations). This forces either continuous double-stepping or constant spinning, which violates natural foot-alternation and is extremely exhausting and repetitive.
The chart repeats the exact same 'D -> JUMP LR' pattern nine times consecutively. This lacks pattern variety and feels very uninspired.

The density suddenly drops off a cliff. The remaining steps land on awkward, highly syncopated 16th-note offsets (e.g., 70.607, 72.108) that feel rhythmically disconnected and jar players accustomed to the previous beat grid.
